Why Gallo?

Woodbridge's little temple of churrasco

Four things we've refused to change since the day we lit the first coals.

Charcoal, never gas

Real embers, real smoke, real crackle. The grill gets lit before we do anything else each morning.

Piri-piri made in-house

Peppers, garlic, lemon, and a family ratio we won't write down. Bottled fresh, brushed on hot.

Portions like avó's

A large here is a large. Nobody has ever left Gallo wondering where the rest of their rice went.

Grab-and-go hot table

Chicken, ribs, and daily specials ready to box by the small, medium, or very ambitious container.

Fan favourites

The three things everyone orders

Full menu
Best seller

Meio Frango · Half Chicken

Charcoal-grilled, split-crisp skin, brushed with your choice of sauce from suave to fogo.

$17.95

Cult classic

Batatas Assadas · Roasted Potatoes

The famous little round ones — golden, garlicky, and gone before you're out of the parking lot.

$5.49

Sweet finish

Pastéis de Nata · Custard Tarts

$4.95

Choose your fire

Every order gets sauced your way

Our piri-piri comes in four strengths, all made from the same house base — peppers, garlic, lemon, olive oil, and patience. Tap a level to see what you're signing up for. Whatever you pick, we brush it on while the chicken is still crackling.

Bottles of the house sauce are on the counter if you want to take the fire home.

A friendly warmth that shows up on the second bite and stays polite. Our most-ordered level.

Pickup, perfected

Phone call to feast in three steps

1

Call it in

Tell us the bird, the sides, and how brave you're feeling on the piri-piri scale.

2

We grill it fresh

Your order goes over live charcoal and gets sauced while it's still crackling — nothing waits under a heat lamp.

3

Pick up & feast

Boxed hot with a papo seco on top. Dinner, handled — leftovers not guaranteed.

Word of beak

The neighbourhood has spoken

Dinner's already on the grill.

Weekends get busy — call ahead and your order will be boxed, sauced, and waiting with a papo seco on top.

The menu

Straight off the coals

Everything is grilled to order or pulled hot from the table. Prices in CAD; every plate comes with your pick of piri-piri, from suave to fogo.

Order ahead for pickup

Whole chickens sell out most Saturdays — call and we'll have yours boxed and waiting.

Call (905) 653-7474

= a dish with built-in heat — everything else is as spicy as you order it

Quarter Chicken · Dark Perna de frango

$10.95

Leg and thigh, the juiciest quarter on the bird, with skin crisped right over the embers.

Quarter Chicken · White Peito de frango

$11.95

Breast and wing, basted twice so it stays tender all the way to the last forkful.

Popular

Popular

Your quarter (dark or white), any two regular sides, a warm roll, and sauce on everything if you say the word.

$12.95

Grilled boneless thigh on a toasted papo seco with garlic aioli and crunchy slaw.

Comes with a médio kick by default

Chicken Espetada Espetada de frango

$14.95

Skewered with bay leaf, peppers, and onion, grilled until the edges catch. Served over Portuguese rice.

Piri-Piri Wings (12) Asinhas

$15.95

A dozen wings tossed on the grill and doused in the good stuff. Napkins included, dignity not guaranteed.

Quente unless you beg otherwise

Popular

Roasted Potatoes Batatas assadas

$5.49

The famous little round ones, roasted in chicken drippings with garlic. People drive in from Toronto for these.

Fresh-Cut Fries Batatas fritas

$4.99

Cut in the morning, fried when you order. Ask for a drizzle of piri-piri if you know what's good.

Portuguese Rice Arroz à portuguesa

$4.99

Tomato-tinged, buttery, and generous. A large here is a small somewhere else — we've checked.

Garden Salad Salada mista

$5.49

Crisp greens, tomato, onion, and our olive-oil-and-oregano dressing. The token vegetable, done right.

Potato Salad Salada de batata

$5.49

Creamy, tangy, flecked with parsley — made in small batches the way it's always been made.

Garlic Rapini Grelos com alho

$6.49

Sautéed until just tender with a serious amount of garlic and a squeeze of lemon.

Addictive

Spicy Marinated Olives Azeitonas picantes

$4.49

Marinated in-house with chili, garlic, and herbs. Customers have described them as "beyond addictive." They're right.

Gently spicy by design

Portuguese Roll Papo seco

$1.25

Crusty outside, cloud inside. Built for mopping up whatever sauce is left in the box.

Kale & Chouriço Soup Caldo verde

$5.99

Silky potato broth, ribbons of kale, and slices of smoky chouriço. Portugal in a cup.

House Piri-Piri Bottle Molho piri-piri · 250 mL

$7.49

Take the fire home. Choose your strength at the counter; keeps for a month in the fridge, lasts a week in practice.

For the household that can't agree: a whole chicken and a rack of charcoal-grilled ribs, plus two large sides.

$42.95

Wings arrive quente

Catering Trays Hot-table favourites by the tray · 24h notice

From $39.95

Chicken, ribs, rice, potatoes, rapini, and salads by the half or full tray for baptisms, birthdays, and every Portuguese occasion where the food matters more than the speeches. Call us a day ahead and tell us how many mouths.

Popular

Pastéis de Nata (2) Custard tarts

$4.95

Flaky pastry, blistered custard, a shake of cinnamon if you like. Sold in pairs for a reason.

Rice Pudding Arroz doce

$4.75

Creamy, lemon-scented, and dusted with cinnamon in the traditional lattice. Grandmother-grade.

Bola de Berlim Portuguese cream doughnut

$3.95

Pillowy fried dough rolled in sugar and filled with egg custard. Beach-day nostalgia, year-round.

Sumol Orange or passionfruit

$2.95

Portugal's fizzy classic, ice cold. The official pairing for anything with piri-piri on it.

Compal Juice Peach, pear, or mango

$3.25

Thick, fruity, and imported — the juice box every Portuguese kid grew up shaking first.

Bica Espresso

$2.50

Short, strong, and pulled the Lisbon way. Take it standing at the counter for the full effect.

Galão Portuguese latte

$3.75

One part espresso, three parts steamed milk, served tall. Dessert's best friend.

Our story

The rooster came first

Every proper churrasqueira has a rooster over the door. Ours has a name, a legend, and a very strong opinion about how chicken should be grilled.

From Portugal to Weston Road

Gallo started the way most good churrasqueiras do: with a family recipe that was never supposed to be a business. The marinade — garlic, paprika, lemon, white wine, bay leaf — came across the Atlantic in someone's head, not on paper, and it still gets made by feel, in batches big enough for a Woodbridge weekend.

We do things the slow way because there isn't a fast way that tastes right. Chickens rest in that marinade overnight. The charcoal gets lit early, and the first birds hit the grill before the doors open. By noon the hot table is loaded — chicken, ribs, rice, those little roasted potatoes — and the phone starts ringing with pickup orders for dinner.

Most of our regulars we know by order rather than by name: the half-chicken-extra-hot family, the Saturday two-whole-birds crowd, the kids sent in with exact change for pastéis de nata. That's the job, honestly — feeding the neighbourhood like it's our own kitchen table. Because it is.

Bom apetite — see you at the counter.

Why the rooster?

In the old Portuguese legend of Barcelos, a wrongly accused pilgrim swore that the roast rooster on the judge's table would stand up and crow to prove his innocence — and it did. Ever since, the Galo de Barcelos has been Portugal's symbol of good luck, honesty, and justice. We figured a restaurant named after him had better keep its promises: honest food, honest portions, honest fire.

Marinated overnight, every single bird — no exceptions, no express lane.

Charcoal since day one. If the grill isn't crackling, we're not open.

Family-run in Woodbridge — same counter, same faces, same recipes.

Taste the story for yourself

Come find the rooster

We're on Weston Road just north of Rutherford, in Woodbridge. Follow the charcoal smell — it has never once led anyone astray.

Where the smoke rises

Unit 11

In the plaza on the east side, two doors from the corner.

Call for directions
Good to know

Little questions, quick answers

Do you take reservations?

We're take-out first with a few seats inside, so no reservations needed — just come hungry. For big orders, call ahead and we'll have everything boxed and waiting.

How hot is "fogo", really?

Genuinely hot — it's heavy on bird's eye peppers and we don't water it down. If you're unsure, start with quente; you can always add sauce at the counter, but you can't take it off.

Do you cater parties?

Yes — chicken, ribs, and all the sides by the half or full tray with 24 hours' notice. Call (905) 653-7474 and tell us how many mouths.

Where do I park?

Free plaza parking right outside Unit 11 — you can usually smell the grill from your car door.
